title: DSDS Link Definition
description: A typed pointer to an external resource — source code, design files, docs, packages. Every link needs a `kind` and a `url` (or, for the deprecated inter-entity form, an `identifier`). NOTE: links once doubled as inter-entity references via `identifier`; that use is deprecated in favor of the entity's typed `relationships` array. The `identifier`, `role`, and `required` properties remain for backward compatibility only.

link.description: A typed pointer to an external resource. A link points to an external resource by `url` (source code, design files, docs, packages). The `kind` property sorts the reference. Standard external kinds: 'source', 'design', 'storybook', 'documentation', 'package', 'repository', 'manifest' (a machine-readable component manifest, e.g. a Custom Elements Manifest). Custom values are allowed. DEPRECATED — using a link to express how entities relate is deprecated in favor of the entity's `relationships` array, which carries typed, validated edges. This covers the relationship kinds ('alternative', 'parent', 'child', 'related') and internal artifact references by `identifier` ('component', 'token', 'token-group', 'foundation', 'pattern', 'theme'). Those forms still validate for backward compatibility, but tools SHOULD warn (DSDS-010) and authors SHOULD migrate them to `relationships`. Reserve `link` for external resources addressed by `url`.

kind.description: The category of the linked resource. Standard external kinds: 'source', 'design', 'storybook', 'documentation', 'package', 'repository'. Custom values are allowed and MUST match ^[a-z][a-z0-9-]*$ (schema-enforced; all standard kinds already do). DEPRECATED kinds — 'alternative', 'parent', 'child', 'related' (relationship kinds) and 'component', 'token', 'token-group', 'foundation', 'pattern', 'theme' (internal artifact references) are deprecated; express these as typed edges in the entity's `relationships` array instead.

url.description: The URL of the linked resource. MUST be a valid absolute URI. Required for external resource links. Optional for internal artifact references, where `identifier` names the artifact. When both `url` and `identifier` are present, `url` points to the artifact's docs page and `identifier` is the machine-readable cross-reference.

identifier.description: DEPRECATED — the identifier of a referenced DSDS entity. Use the entity's `relationships` array for inter-entity references. Retained for backward compatibility: a link with an `identifier` still follows entityRef resolution rules (it MUST match a documented entity).

label.description: Display text for this link — what a user sees when the link renders (e.g., 'React component source', 'Design file — variants', 'Button component'). Use it for the display name of the resource. To describe an internal artifact's role, use `role` instead.

role.description: The role the referenced entity plays in the current entity (e.g., 'Primary action trigger', 'Provides semantic text color tokens'). Distinct from `label`, which is display text for the link itself. When omitted, the link is a general association.

required.description: DEPRECATED — whether the referenced artifact is required for the parent entity to work. Use `required` on a `relationships` edge instead. Retained for backward compatibility on internal artifact references. Defaults to false.
